content.summary: Detects musical beats from a live audio input using a chain of CHOP operators. An Audio Device In captures the signal, an Analyze CHOP extracts RMS or peak levels, and a combination of Filter, Math, and Logic CHOPs isolate beat onsets. The resulting trigger channel can drive visual parameters, animations, or scene changes in sync with the music.

codeSnippets.label: Configure beat detection chain parameters
codeSnippets.code:
# Set up a basic beat detection chain
audio_in = op('audiodevicein1')
analyze = op('analyze1')
lag = op('lag1')

# Analyze RMS of the audio
analyze.par.function = 'rms'

# Lag CHOP as envelope follower
# Short attack, longer release to capture transients
lag.par.lag1 = 0.005   # 5ms attack
lag.par.lag2 = 0.1     # 100ms release

# The difference between instant level and lagged envelope
# gives you onset detection when positive
codeSnippets.description: Configures the CHOP chain for onset detection by comparing instantaneous RMS against a lagged envelope.

tips: Use an Audio Band EQ CHOP before analysis to isolate kick drum frequencies (60-150 Hz) for more reliable beat detection.
Adjust the lag/filter times based on the tempo range — faster music needs shorter release times.
Add a Speed CHOP to count zero-crossings on the beat trigger for BPM estimation.
Use a Select CHOP to pick only the left channel if working with stereo input.
